title: Creamy Scrambled Eggs
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Gordon Ramsay-style creamy scrambled eggs: soft curds, butter, exact TM6 and TM7 settings and a café-worthy breakfast method.
intro: Ramsay's trick is low heat and pulling the eggs while they're still glossy. The Thermomix's gentle 90°C and butterfly whisk make them silky every time.
steps: Insert the butterfly whisk. Add the eggs and butter to the mixing bowl. Cook 7 min / 90°C / speed 2, watching closely — stop the moment they're softly set and still glossy.
Add the crème fraîche, salt and pepper. Mix 15 sec / 0°C / speed 2 — this stops the cooking and keeps them silky.
Serve straight away on hot sourdough, scattered with chives; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Pull them while slightly loose — the residual heat finishes them.
The low 90°C is the secret to creamy, never rubbery, eggs.

faq.a: Gentle heat keeps the curds soft and creamy instead of dry and rubbery.
Yes — it folds the eggs slowly so they stay silky.

faq.q: Is this the original Gordon Ramsay recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Gordon Ramsay and scrambled eggs; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
